scope: Skill invocation lifecycle across the model-facing registry, runtime skill manager, and TUI skill tool.

requiredBehaviors: registers the model-facing Skill tool in the canonical runtime tool registry
keeps Skill visible by default as the skill invocation surface
maps raw string dispatch arguments to the Skill tool's skill field
edgeCases: SkillCreate is not a separate model-facing registry alias; creating a skill remains a skill-file lifecycle handled by registered skill roots
raw string arguments invoke the named skill instead of failing schema parsing
the registry preserves Skill as a side-effecting tool because invocation mutates session skill state
retired delegation aliases remain rejected while Skill stays explicitly registered

requiredBehaviors: loads skill content through the session skills manager
passes optional arguments through to skill rendering
records successful skill invocations for compaction and session continuity
rejects skills that disable model invocation
edgeCases: leading slashes are normalized away before lookup
unknown skills return an actionable error with available skill names
model-disabled skills return an error before content is emitted

requiredBehaviors: validates missing and malformed skill inputs with actionable messages
merges local and MCP-derived skill commands for invocation lookup
renders successful skill loads and skill progress messages in the TUI
applies skill-scoped allowed tools, model, effort, and forked-agent execution metadata
edgeCases: plugin skill names render without requiring command metadata
prompt commands that are not model-invocable are rejected
forked skills clear their agent-scoped invoked-skill state after completion
